docs.first_paragraph: kolm publishes to its own hub by default (`kolm publish foo.kolm`), but the .kolm format is designed to round-trip through any object store. This page documents the three most common BYO patterns. The artifact's signature + receipt travel inside the zip — recipients can always verify provenance with `kolm verify` regardless of how the bytes arrived.

docs.title: Cloud Compute Broker
docs.first_paragraph: The broker is the backend decision contract for users who do not want to think in provider names. They describe the workload, privacy boundary, artifact size, dataset size, budget, and storage state; Kolm returns the safest executable compute path or the exact missing configuration.

docs.title: Kolm Cloud Product Readiness
docs.first_paragraph: This is the production contract for teams that do not have local GPUs or need enterprise deployment controls. The product stays artifact-first: cloud compute is a pluggable compile/train/serve substrate, while `.kolm` remains the signed portable output.

docs.title: Compliance Certification Packet
docs.first_paragraph: This packet is the local evidence spine for compliance review. It does not claim live certification. It records which implementation-control surfaces exist and which third-party artifacts still have to be attached before public trust copy can say SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP, SLSA, or SBOM evidence is complete.

docs.title: Distill Strategy Oracle
docs.first_paragraph: The distillation strategy oracle decides whether Kolm should train at all, and if it should, which objective is justified by the evidence. It prevents the bad product behavior where every workflow is pushed into a generic fine-tune.

docs.first_paragraph: Agent workloads — calls to get_weather , search_docs , create_ticket , query_database — are mostly repetitive. kolm.ai captures the tool-use trace alongside the prompt and response, then teaches a smaller student model to make the same tool calls locally. docs.title: Connect Anthropic · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: The kolm.ai proxy serves the Anthropic /v1/messages surface on the same port as the OpenAI surface. Set ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL , leave the rest of your SDK alone, and every messages.create lands in the local lake before forwarding to api.anthropic.com.

docs.title: Connect Gemini · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: Google AI Studio ships an OpenAI-compatible surface at generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai . Point the kolm.ai proxy at it, point your OpenAI SDK at the proxy, and Gemini calls land in your local lake just like any other provider.

docs.title: Connect OpenAI · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: The kolm.ai local proxy speaks the OpenAI wire format natively. Point your existing OpenAI SDK, LangChain, or raw fetch at http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1 and every chat.completions , responses , and embeddings call gets mirrored into the local lake before forwarding upstream. No SDK rewrite, no proxy changes, no streaming break.

docs.title: Connect OpenRouter · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: OpenRouter exposes 100+ frontier and open-weight models behind one OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Because the kolm.ai proxy speaks the OpenAI wire format, OpenRouter just works: set OPENAI_BASE_URL at the local proxy, set OPENAI_API_KEY to your OpenRouter key, and the proxy forwards to openrouter.ai/api/v1 on your behalf.

docs.title: Connectors · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: Every framework you already use speaks an OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic-compatible API. Point its base URL at the local kolm proxy on http://127.0.0.1:8787 and every round-trip is captured, the response streams through unchanged, and you build a private corpus you can later compile into a smaller, cheaper artifact.

docs.title: Datasets · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: A dataset in kolm.ai is a deterministic, frozen view over the event lake. You pick a namespace, you pick a window, you split by sha256_mod for guaranteed disjointness, you label what needs labelling, and you freeze. Every training and eval run pins to a dataset id so results are reproducible months later.

You can hand it to anyone with the runtime and they get the same outputs you do.

docs.title: LangChain JS · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: LangChain JS speaks OpenAI and Anthropic over their published HTTP APIs. Pass configuration.baseURL (OpenAI) or clientOptions.baseURL (Anthropic) to point the chain at the local kolm proxy. Every call into the chain becomes a captured (input, output) pair you can later distill.

docs.title: LangChain Python · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: LangChain Python wraps the official openai and anthropic Python SDKs. Pass base_url= on construction (or set the env var) and the chain talks to the local kolm proxy instead of the upstream provider. Streaming, tool calls, and structured output pass through unchanged.

docs.title: LlamaIndex JS · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: LlamaIndex.TS wraps the official openai and anthropic SDKs under the hood. Pass additionalSessionOptions on the LLM constructor to override the base URL and your chat engines, query engines, and agents all flow through the local kolm proxy. Captures land in the named namespace; thresholds gate kolm distill .

docs.title: LlamaIndex Python · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: LlamaIndex Python uses the official openai and anthropic Python SDKs internally. Pass api_base= on the LLM constructor and every higher-level abstraction (query engine, chat engine, agent) follows. Set Settings.llm once and the rest of the pipeline routes through the local kolm proxy automatically.

docs.title: OpenTelemetry semantic conventions · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: kolm emits a stable set of kolm.* OpenTelemetry attributes and a four-phase inference span timeline (queue → load → prefill → decode) so token-confidence, routing decisions, and K-Score drift show up natively in Honeycomb, Grafana Tempo, Datadog, and Jaeger. The exporter version is pinned by OTEL_W733_VERSION (currently w733-v1 ). When no OTel tracer is registered, kolm is an honest

docs.title: Prometheus and Grafana · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: Every kolm runtime exposes a /metrics endpoint in Prometheus text format. Six pre-registered metrics cover capture rate, load-queue depth, GPU memory by tier, kernel selection, speculative-decoding acceptance, and HTTP latency. Drop the bundled dashboards/kolm-runtime.json into Grafana and you have a working observability stack in two minutes.

docs.title: Privacy guide · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: Every prompt and response that goes through the proxy is scanned by 17 detector classes before it is written to the lake. You set the per-class policy in ~/.kolm/runtime/policy.json . Detected spans are replaced with VAR_* placeholders, blocked classes 4xx the request, and the full audit trail is appended to ~/.kolm/runtime/audit.log .

list() return a snapshot of the locally hydrated recipes
run(name, input) execute one recipe on this device, return signed receipt
runAll(input) execute every registry recipe against one input
verified(opts) Lane 2 verified inference: sample k frontier candidates, return the verified winner
Provider capture base URLs Lane 3: proxy upstream SDK calls and record captures
wrap(client, opts) proxy your existing AI SDK client and route messages.create through verified inference
verifyReceipt(receipt, input, output) round-trip check a receipt against its declared input and output

docs.title: Training · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: Training in kolm.ai is plan-first. You hand the planner a frozen dataset, the planner detects the task kind from row shape, picks a path profile, picks a backbone, prints the GPU-minute budget and the expected score, and waits for your approval. Then it runs.

docs.title: Troubleshooting · kolm.ai

headings.text: Unauthorized. The API key is missing, expired, or wrong tenant.
Forbidden. The key is valid but lacks the required role or scope.
Rate limited. You exceeded the per-key per-minute quota.
The durable capture store is offline. The request was rejected, not silently dropped.
The receipt could not be cross-checked with the witness in time.
BYOC attestation failure. The cluster nodes did not produce a TEE quote the host trusts.
Trainer timeout or OOM. The student model did not fit or the step budget was exhausted.
Compile refuses with "dataset empty". The capture filter matched zero rows.
Compile blocks on K-score below gate. The artifact would not pass production gates.
Quantize says torch not installed. The Python sidecar is missing.
kolm verify returns 200 with verified: false. The chain has a broken link.
SSE /v1/capture/stream disconnects every few minutes. Idle timeout from a proxy or CDN.

docs.first_paragraph: Eleven common failures with the kolm.ai CLI, API, and runtime. Each entry names the symptom, the root cause, and three to five concrete steps to recover. If a fix is not here, open an issue or email support@kolm.ai with the receipt id from kolm logs .

docs.title: Verify guide · kolm.ai
docs.first_paragraph: kolm verify <artifact.kolm> re-hashes every bundled file, re-checks the Ed25519 signature, refuses on train/hold-out leakage, missing binary, or synthetic-only seeds. One CLI command, six closed-enum failure codes, offline by default.

docs.first_paragraph: Every POST carries X-Kolm-Signature: t=<unix-time>,v1=<hmac-sha256-hex> . Compute the HMAC over t.body with your webhook secret and compare in constant time. Reject any request older than five minutes.
